questions.prompt: Cheapest entry point for incrementality testing on Google?

choices.text: Brand search suppression test. Pause your branded Search campaign for 2-3 weeks (or use Drafts & Experiments at 50/50 split), track branded organic clicks (should rise as paid vanishes) and total revenue. Compare paid revenue lost vs organic revenue gained. Common finding: 60-90% of brand search revenue is non-incremental — users typing your brand were going to find you regardless. The cheapest, highest-value incrementality lesson most accounts can run. Don't kill brand search after the test; right-size it.

questions.explanation: Brand search suppression is the cheap, high-value entry point.

questions.prompt: Lift test shows top-of-funnel YouTube at 1.6x incremental ROAS, brand Search at 0.4x. Reallocation?

choices.text: Move budget from brand Search to YouTube. Brand Search at 0.4x incremental means 60% of its credited revenue would have happened anyway via organic — heavily overcredited. YouTube at 1.6x is delivering genuine new revenue. Shift 30-50% of brand Search budget to YouTube and retest in 6-12 months. Don't kill brand Search entirely — some incremental contribution remains (especially against competitor brand bidding), but right-size it to the real causal contribution. Update tROAS targets to reflect true incremental cost.

questions.explanation: Reallocate toward incremental winners. Brand commonly over-credited; YouTube under.
